summarize-for-public.summaryTitle: What the Public Should Know About GIA
summarize-for-public.summary: Global Infrastructure Advisory (GIA) is a next‑generation platform designed to support communities, contractors, farmers, and governments with clear, AI‑powered guidance for building and maintaining critical infrastructure. The system uses a secure, multi‑zone AI engine (Deep Mind 2100) to deliver trusted insights while protecting sensitive data.
publicCapabilities: Explore infrastructure topics in simple language
Access tools for land, water, agriculture, and community planning
View safe, high‑level maps and development previews
Learn how infrastructure connects to agriculture, energy, and rural development
Use public‑safe calculators (soil, water, crop windows, livestock basics)
Understand how GIA supports contractors, farmers, and local governments

Disaster Deployment Engine.purpose: Coordinate global disaster response across infrastructure, energy, water, transport, public services, contractors, NGOs, and government.

outputs.deploymentPlan: who goes where, when, and with what resources
outputs.microgridDirectives: island mode, load shedding, battery strategy

anys-worker.name: ANYSWorker
anys-worker.purpose: Act as a controlled adapter between the Collective and any external system, sector, or scale. Translate, normalize, and mediate interactions while strictly enforcing MCP, DIDs, verifiable credentials, and sovereign policy boundaries.

MCP_protocol_layer.description: All interactions between external systems and the Collective are mediated through MCP via ANYS.

DID_identity.description: External systems interfacing through ANYS must be represented by a DID wrapper identity.

VC_trust_credentials.description: Verifiable Credentials define what each external system is allowed to see, request, or influence.

Sovereign_policy_layer.description: ANYS enforces the strictest interpretation of sovereign policy for all external interactions.

discoverExternalSystems.description: Identify external systems requesting or requiring integration with the Collective.

classifyExternalSystem.description: Classify external systems by type, risk level, sector, and jurisdiction.

wrapExternalIdentity.description: Issue or bind wrapper DIDs to external systems for controlled interaction.

validateExternalCredentials.description: Validate external VCs and determine allowed operations and trust mappings.

mapTrustZones.description: Map external systems into appropriate internal trust zones or sandbox zones.

translateProtocols.description: Translate external protocols (REST, SOAP, legacy, custom) into MCP-compatible messages.

normalizeData.description: Normalize external data into internal schemas with strict validation.

enforceAccessControl.description: Decide what external systems can read, write, or trigger inside the Collective.

logAllExternalFlows.description: Audit all external interactions for traceability and compliance.

cyber-worker.name: Cyber Worker
cyber-worker.description: Handles cyber incidents, telemetry, threat intelligence, and escalation.

ewo-builder.incident_description: Complete loss of service from tower affecting rural West Virginia coverage.
ewo-builder.required_action: Dispatch tower crew, restore power/backhaul, verify coverage.
ewo-builder.primary_objective: Restore minimum viable service to affected West Virginia region.
secondary_objectives: Log root cause for post-incident review.

regulatory.notes: Used for cyber incident classification, cost estimation, and response planning across all sectors.

economics.notes: Global economic parameters for infra cost modeling

rules.description: Prevent unsafe frequency excursions.
rules.condition: data.gridFrequency < ai.globalCode.energy.grid.minFrequency || data.gridFrequency > ai.globalCode.energy.grid.maxFrequency || ai.giiContext.systemicRiskScore > 0.7 || ai.autonomy.level < 2

rules.description: Rapid frequency deviation requires human review.
Prevent unsafe bus voltage levels.
rules.condition: data.busVoltage < ai.globalCode.energy.grid.minVoltageKv || data.busVoltage > ai.globalCode.energy.grid.maxVoltageKv || ai.giiContext.sectorRiskScore > 0.75 || ai.autonomy.level < 1

rules.description: Prevent line overload beyond thermal rating.
Emergency loading requires human review.
Prevent unintentional islanding.
rules.condition: data.isIslandingRisk == true && action.name == "OpenBreaker" || ai.giiContext.cascadingFailureRisk == true || ai.autonomy.level < 2

rules.description: Critical bus switching requires human review.
Blackstart restricted to deepgov/system with high autonomy.
Prevent pipeline overpressure beyond safe operating limits.
Rapid pressure change indicates potential leak and requires human review.
Detected leak requires immediate shutdown.
Prevent unsafe flow rate exceeding pipeline design.
Prevent unsafe temperature conditions in pipeline or pump station.
Corrosion risk above threshold requires human review.
Critical valve operations require human review.
rules.condition: action.name == "OpenValve" && data.valveCritical == true || ai.giiContext.cascadingFailureRisk == true || ai.autonomy.level < 2

rules.description: Unauthorized valve operations are denied.
Emergency shutoff allowed only when leak or rupture is detected.
rules.condition: action.name == "EmergencyShutoff" && data.leakDetected != true && data.ruptureDetected != true || ai.autonomy.level < 2

rules.description: Pipeline rupture requires immediate shutdown.
SCADA control of pipeline requires gov, deepgov, or system trust zone with sufficient autonomy.
Unauthorized remote access to pipeline control systems is denied.
Anomalous behavior in pipeline telemetry requires human review.
Environmental protection: prevent operations during high spill risk.
Only certified pipeline technicians may perform maintenance.
Prevent construction that violates zoning classification.
Zoning variances require human review.
Prevent construction when structural load exceeds global safe limits.
Detected structural defect requires immediate review.
Prevent occupancy if fire suppression systems are offline.
Fire alarm faults require human review.
Prevent unsafe electrical load beyond panel rating.
Electrical system faults require human review.
Prevent occupancy if plumbing system is non-functional.
Prevent unsafe HVAC operations during extreme temperatures.
Prevent occupancy if ADA accessibility requirements are not met.
Inspections must be performed by certified inspectors.
Construction or modification requires an approved permit.
Permit overrides require human review.
Demolition requires structural clearance and safety checks.
Rural housing systems require priority protection and review.
Unauthorized remote access to smart building systems is denied.
Critical building automation systems require gov, deepgov, or system trust zone.
Anomalous behavior in building telemetry requires human review.

infrastructure-manifest.description: Unified Global Infrastructure Manifest for GIA V12-Alpha

rules.description: Prevent unsafe tower operations during high wind or structural instability.
Only certified tower technicians may perform tower maintenance.
Prevent RF output exceeding global safe limits.
Prevent unsafe fiber splice or cut operations without authorization.
Fiber signal loss above global threshold requires human review.
Prevent microwave backhaul misalignment beyond safe tolerance.
Backhaul congestion above critical threshold requires human review.
Satellite ground station operations require gov, deepgov, or system trust zone.
Emergency 911 traffic must be prioritized over all other routing.
Public safety network operations require human review during outages.
Rural broadband systems require priority protection and review.
Prevent unsafe temperature or power conditions in telecom data centers.
rules.condition: data.tempC > ai.globalCode.telecom.datacenter.maxTempC || data.powerLoadPercent > ai.globalCode.telecom.datacenter.maxPowerLoadPercent || ai.giiContext.cascadingFailureRisk == true || ai.autonomy.level < 2

rules.description: Unauthorized remote access to telecom control systems is denied.
Critical telecom SCADA/NOC operations require gov, deepgov, or system trust zone.
Anomalous behavior in telecom telemetry requires human review.
Emergency shutoff of telecom systems allowed only during verified cyber attack or physical attack.
Prevent unsafe high-voltage operations outside global safe limits.
rules.condition: data.voltageKv < ai.globalCode.energy.grid.minVoltageKv || data.voltageKv > ai.globalCode.energy.grid.maxVoltageKv || ai.giiContext.sectorRiskScore > 0.75 || ai.autonomy.level < 2

rules.description: Prevent transformer overload beyond thermal rating.
Emergency transformer loading requires human review.
Prevent breaker operations that risk arc-flash or unsafe switching.
Critical breaker operations require human review.
Prevent switching that causes unintentional islanding.
rules.condition: data.isIslandingRisk == true && action.name == "OpenBreaker" || ai.giiContext.cascadingFailureRisk == true || ai.autonomy.level < 2

rules.description: Topology changes on critical buses require human review.
Detected line or transformer fault requires immediate isolation.
Prevent transformer overheating beyond global safe temperature.
Ground faults require immediate human review.
SCADA control requires gov, deepgov, or system trust zone with sufficient autonomy.
Unauthorized remote access to transmission control systems is denied.
Critical transmission SCADA/NOC operations require gov, deepgov, or system trust zone.
Anomalous behavior in transmission telemetry requires human review.
Only certified high-voltage technicians may perform maintenance.

policy-manifest.description: Unified Sovereign Policy Manifest for GIA V12-Alpha

common-rule.title: Federal Policy for the Protection of Human Research Subjects
common-rule.authority: Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), Office for Human Research Protections (OHRP)
common-rule.scope: GIA workflows involving human research subjects or identifiable human data
common-rule.description: Establishes protections for human research subjects including consent, review, and risk assessment requirements

principles.title: Respect for Persons
principles.description: Human subjects must be treated as autonomous agents capable of deliberation and choice

principles.title: Beneficence
principles.description: Maximize benefits and minimize harms to research subjects

principles.title: Justice
principles.description: Benefits and burdens of research must be fairly distributed

principles.title: Informed Consent
principles.description: Subjects must provide voluntary, informed consent before research participation

principles.title: IRB Review
principles.description: Research involving human subjects must receive Institutional Review Board approval

minimal_risk.description: No greater risk than ordinary life

greater_than_minimal_risk.description: Risk of harm greater than everyday activities

controls.description: Block research workflows without IRB approval documentation
Verify informed consent document compliance
Monitor data access for consent compliance
Annual continuing review enforcement

dhs-compliance.title: Department of Homeland Security Compliance and Computer Matching Programs
dhs-compliance.authority: Department of Homeland Security (DHS), Office of Compliance
dhs-compliance.scope: GIA infrastructure protection and computer-assisted data matching operations
dhs-compliance.description: Establishes DHS compliance standards for critical infrastructure operations and data matching between federal systems

principles.title: Critical Infrastructure Protection
principles.description: All infrastructure operations must maintain security and operational resilience

principles.title: Computer Matching Agreement
principles.description: Formal CMA required before matching data between systems

principles.title: Notice and Validation
principles.description: Data matching must include accuracy verification and subject notification

principles.title: Data Quality Standards
principles.description: Source data must meet accuracy and completeness standards

principles.title: Security Incident Reporting
principles.description: All security incidents must be reported to DHS

controls.description: Block data matching without signed CMA
Verify data accuracy before use
Monitor infrastructure operations for anomalies
Log all critical infrastructure access

references: Computer Matching and Privacy Protection Act of 1988 (5 USC 552a)
OMB Circular A-108 (Federal Agency Responsibilities for Review, Reporting, and Publication)

nces-ethics.title: Department of Education NCES — The Forum Guide to Data Ethics
nces-ethics.authority: National Center for Education Statistics (NCES), Department of Education
nces-ethics.scope: GIA workforce development, human capital data, and educational infrastructure programs
nces-ethics.description: Provides ethical standards for handling sensitive human data in educational and workforce contexts

principles.title: Respect for Individuals
principles.description: Recognize the dignity and autonomy of data subjects

principles.title: Beneficence
principles.description: Ensure data use produces net social benefit

principles.title: Justice in Data Distribution
principles.description: Ensure benefits and burdens of data use are fairly distributed

principles.title: Transparency
principles.description: Be transparent about data collection, use, and limitations

principles.title: Accountability
principles.description: Take responsibility for ethical consequences of data use

controls.description: Audit all workforce data processing for discrimination
Verify data minimization in collection
Monitor for algorithmic bias in hiring/promotion recommendations
Enforce purpose-limitation in data use

violationScenarios.violation: Using sensitive data without stated purpose

references: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)

oge-misconduct-reporting.title: Office of Government Ethics (OGE) — Where to Report Misconduct and Conflict of Interest
oge-misconduct-reporting.authority: Office of Government Ethics (OGE), Executive Branch
oge-misconduct-reporting.scope: GIA employee conduct, conflict of interest disclosure, and misconduct reporting
oge-misconduct-reporting.description: Establishes mandatory reporting requirements for government employees and contractors regarding ethics violations, misconduct, and conflicts of interest

principles.title: Public Service Integrity
principles.description: All employees must prioritize public interest over personal gain

principles.title: Conflict of Interest Avoidance
principles.description: Employees must disclose and recuse from situations with conflicts

principles.title: Mandatory Reporting
principles.description: Suspected misconduct must be reported immediately

principles.title: Protected Reporting
principles.description: Reporters must be protected from retaliation

principles.title: Transparency and Accountability
principles.description: Ethics violations must be investigated and acted upon

conflictOfInterestTypes.description: Stocks, partnerships, loans, gifts in regulated companies
Gifts, meals, travel from prohibited sources
Negotiations with companies involved in current decisions

controls.description: Mandatory ethics training for all employees
Automated conflict of interest screening
Anonymous reporting hotline
Retaliation protection monitoring
Investigation tracking and case management

omb-m14-06.title: OMB Memorandum M-14-06 — Guidance for Providing and Using Administrative Data for Statistical Purposes
omb-m14-06.scope: GIA use of administrative data for agriculture, contractor, and workforce analytics
omb-m14-06.description: Establishes standards for federal agencies using administrative data for statistical and research purposes

principles.title: Legal Authority
principles.description: Statistical use of administrative data must have clear legal authority

principles.title: Transparency and Confidentiality
principles.description: Statistical uses must be transparent; confidentiality of individual records must be protected

principles.title: Data Quality
principles.description: Administrative data must meet defined quality standards for statistical use

principles.title: Purpose Limitation
principles.description: Data collected for administrative purposes may only be used for statistical purposes with safeguards

principles.title: Data Governance
principles.description: Clear data governance structures must define roles and responsibilities

controls.description: Verify legal authority for each administrative data use
De-identify data when possible before statistical analysis
Prevent disclosure of individual records in statistical products
Maintain clear audit trail of all data access and use

omb-m20-12.title: Phase 4 Implementation of the Evidence Act: Program Evaluation Standards and Practices
omb-m20-12.scope: GIA evaluation methodology and evidence standards for infrastructure programs
omb-m20-12.description: Establishes evidence standards for program evaluation, ensuring GIA recommendations are grounded in rigorous, unbiased analysis

principles.title: Rigorous Evaluation Design
principles.description: All program evaluations must use recognized methodologies (RCT, quasi-experimental, observational)

principles.title: Bias Assessment
principles.description: All evaluations must document potential biases and mitigation strategies

principles.title: Evidence Quality Tiering
principles.description: Evaluate and tier evidence by quality level (Tier 1: RCT, Tier 2: Quasi-exp, Tier 3: Observational)

principles.title: Evaluation Planning
principles.description: Comprehensive evaluation plans required before program launch

principles.title: Learning Agenda Development
principles.description: All agencies must maintain evidence-building learning agendas

controls.description: Verify evaluation plan approved before program execution
Validate bias assessment documentation
Peer review of evaluation methodology
Block publication of unvetted evaluation results

violationScenarios.violation: Publishing evaluation results without methodology documentation
Evaluation sample size below statistical minimum

ostp-scientific-integrity.title: OSTP Scientific Integrity Policy
ostp-scientific-integrity.authority: Executive Office of the President, Office of Science and Technology Policy
ostp-scientific-integrity.scope: Global Infrastructure Advisory AI systems and scientific operations
ostp-scientific-integrity.description: Ensures all GIA operations maintain scientific integrity, transparency, and freedom from political interference

principles.title: Transparency and Openness
principles.description: Methods must be transparent, reproducible, and openly documented

principles.title: Data Integrity
principles.description: Data must not be fabricated, suppressed, or misrepresented

principles.title: Freedom from Political Pressure
principles.description: Scientific conclusions must not be altered for political purposes

principles.title: Peer Review
principles.description: High-impact research findings must undergo independent review

principles.title: Conflict of Interest Disclosure
principles.description: All personnel must disclose conflicts that could bias analysis

controls.description: All analytical methods must be documented and auditable
Data sources must be cited and verifiable
High-impact decisions require review by System trust zone
Temporal data manipulation is prohibited

integration-map.summary: Unified integration map for all worker, backend, voice, mission, and runtime call-sites into Seven/autonomous.

components.evidence_snippet: const result = await processAIRequest({ text: "auth login", ... }, env );
const result = await VoiceBus.dispatch({ text: message, role, sessionId, sensors, resources });
const mission = orchestrateMission({ text, role, sessionId, sensors, resources });
const stack = new SevenStack(terrain, { speak: (msg) => console.log('[Seven Voice]', msg) });
return orchestrateMission({ text, role, sessionId, sensors, resources });
SevenRuntime class and adapters (seven.ts, seven-stack.ts, safety/api, interop)

notes: This integration map is consumed by Seven’s routing engine.
Each component entry defines a call-site into Seven/autonomous.
Seven uses this map to build the full routing graph and detect missing bindings.

Trust + policy gate.name: ExpansionWorker
Trust + policy gate.purpose: Expand the Collective safely across clouds, regions, satellites, and infrastructure zones while enforcing trust, sovereignty, and policy boundaries.
Trust + policy gate.recursionRule: Each new iteration re-enters coreCycle using nextIterationPlan. No nested cycles. No infinite recursion.
